summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #542 where GraphQL `sessionKey` parameter was being ignored for chat.send/history operations, causing all GraphQL clients to default to the 'main' session.
summary.outcome: Made `sessionKey` a required `String` parameter across all GraphQL chat mutations, queries, and subscriptions; regenerated the iOS GraphQL schema; added 3 integration tests; committed and pushed a PR.

repo: Session resolution uses a priority chain: explicit sessionKey > _conn_id-based active session map > 'main' fallback. GraphQL resolvers lack _conn_id injection, so omitting sessionKey always fell to 'main'.
RPC/WebSocket (web UI) injects `_conn_id` via core.rs:540, enabling implicit session resolution. GraphQL callers are programmatic and must always supply sessionKey explicitly.
iOS app uses WebSocket RPC for all chat operations — not GraphQL mutations — so it was unaffected at runtime but its bundled schema file was stale.
There is a dedicated `moltis-schema-export` crate that regenerates `apps/ios/GraphQL/Schema/schema.graphqls` from Rust resolver definitions. Schema must be regenerated after GraphQL type changes.
All broadcast calls in the chat service already consistently include `sessionKey` in the event payload — subscription filtering was sound once the send-side session resolution was corrected.

code.finding: _conn_id is injected into RPC params here, enabling the connection-based session fallback that GraphQL resolvers lack.
Changed session_key from Option<String> to String on send, abort, cancel_queued, clear, compact mutations.
Changed session_key from Option<String> to String on history, context, raw_prompt, full_context queries.
Changed session_key from Option<String> to String on chatEvent subscription; also had a clippy lint (nested if that should be collapsed with &&).
Existing tests already passed sessionKey so they continued to work after the type change. 3 new tests were added verifying that omitting sessionKey returns a GraphQL error.

workflow: Used a beads issue tracker (CLI tool) to create, claim, and close the issue alongside the GitHub issue — internal issue tracking is separate from GitHub.
Schema changes in Rust GraphQL resolvers require running `moltis-schema-export` to keep the iOS bundled schema in sync.
Clippy must be run after edits — it caught a nested-if style issue in subscriptions/mod.rs that fmt did not catch.

friction: Multiple redundant reads of mutations/mod.rs (read twice in two chunks) suggesting the file was larger than initially anticipated.
Multiple Grep iterations were needed to locate all broadcast call sites — the pattern `broadcast(` required progressive refinement.
Clippy lint found after formatting was already run clean, requiring an additional edit-verify cycle.
open_items: This is a breaking GraphQL schema change — any third-party or custom GraphQL clients must be updated to always pass sessionKey. No migration path or deprecation period was implemented.
The subscription event filtering logic (when event payload has no sessionKey it passes through unfiltered) was noted as a potential issue but not addressed as part of this fix.
No end-to-end test was added covering the full path: GraphQL send → event broadcast → subscription filter matching on the same sessionKey.
